questions.question: What is the main objective of Verilog HDL in implementing a Decimal adder?
options: To reduce the power consumption of the circuit
To increase the speed of the circuit
questions.explanation: Verilog HDL is used to design digital circuits. In this context, Verilog HDL is used to implement a decimal adder.

questions.question: Which of the following is a 4-bit decimal adder?
questions.explanation: A 4-bit decimal adder is a digital circuit that performs addition of two 4-bit decimal numbers.

questions.question: What is the function of the 'end' keyword in Verilog HDL?
options: To denote the start of a module
To denote the end of a module
To denote the start of a variable
To denote the end of a variable
questions.explanation: The 'end' keyword in Verilog HDL is used to denote the end of a module.

questions.question: How many stages are required to implement a 4-bit decimal adder using the Half-Carry approach?
questions.explanation: A 4-bit decimal adder using the Half-Carry approach requires 4 stages to implement.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a Decimal adder?
options: The output can be more than 9
questions.explanation: A Decimal adder's output can be 0 or 1, depending on the sum of the two decimal numbers.
